questions.question: Write a C++ code snippet to demonstrate the proper use of try-catch blocks for handling exceptions. Ensure that the try block throws an exception, and the catch block handles it correctly.
questions.hint: Example usage: using std::runtime_error, std::cout, and std::cerr

questions.question: Compare and contrast the difference between throwing a base-class exception versus a derived-class exception in C++. Provide examples to illustrate your answer.
questions.hint: Focus on the benefits and drawbacks of each approach

questions.question: Describe the benefits of using a combination of catch(...) and catch (std::exception) blocks in exception handling. Provide an example code snippet to demonstrate this.
questions.hint: Explaining the importance of handling specific exceptions versus general exceptions
